tags.marine_life: cuttlefish, helmet shells, leopard catsharks, soles, red fanworms, anemones, feather stars, nudibranchs, reef fish
tags.notes: Difficulty upgraded to Intermediate: cold water (12-18°C), kelp navigation required, surge possible. Eagle rays NOT confirmed at this specific site.

tags.marine_life: Cape fur seals, bronze whaler sharks; great white sharks absent since approximately 2017-2018 (documented decline due to orca predation)
tags.notes: Great white sharks disappeared from False Bay circa 2017-2018 per peer-reviewed research (Frontiers in Marine Science, 2025). Cage diving operations now primarily encounter bronze whalers. Description must reflect current status.

Five scuttled vessels; scuttling spanned 1972-1983 (NOT 'early 1970s' alone). Correct spelling: MFV Orotava (not Oratava). SAS Transvaal and SAS Good Hope are Loch-class frigates. Advanced certification required.

tags.marine_life: Cape fur seals, pyjama sharks, spotted gully sharks, Cape knifejaw, Roman sea bream, nudibranchs, sea cucumbers, brittle stars, octopus
tags.notes: Sevengill sharks listed as 'elusive since 2016' per diveteam.co.za; removed as reliable sighting. Great whites also absent from False Bay since ~2018.

Difficulty upgraded to Intermediate: cold Atlantic water, open-water conditions, exposed to swell. Also accessible by shore from North Windmill Beach but most commonly boat dive from Miller's Point (~4km).

tags.location: Hout Bay area, Atlantic seaboard (NOT Table Bay)
tags.marine_life: nudibranchs, basket stars, brittle stars, colonial ascidians, dreadlocks hydroids, sponges, soft corals, cape rock lobster; seals occasional
tags.notes: Located ~4.5km from Hout Bay Harbour on Atlantic seaboard. Previous description incorrectly said 'Table Bay'. Pyjama sharks NOT confirmed at this site — removed.

tags.features: granite caves, swim-throughs, narrow gaps, overhangs, noble coral cavern
tags.marine_life: Cape sea urchins, dark sea cucumbers, spiny starfish, cape rock lobster, sponges, soft corals, noble corals (pink and purple in prize cavern), sea fans, ascidians
tags.conditions: strong swell in southwest winds, currents at passages
tags.visibility_m: up to 20m on good summer days after SE wind
tags.anchor: large iron anchor from Het Huis te Kraaienstein VOC wreck
tags.notes: Depth range 8-17m (centre 5m, seaward edge to 17m). Site is a rocky reef with cave/cavern features; properly classified as cave for its celebrated cavern and swim-throughs.

tags.history: Scuttled to form artificial reef; laid down 20 Jan 1944 Harland & Wolff Belfast, launched 2 Aug 1944
tags.condition: deteriorating; bow broken off and fallen to starboard

tags.features: large boulders, swim-throughs, breaks surface at low tide
tags.marine_life: Red bait ascidian, kelp, false corals, feather stars, sea cucumbers, fish shoals, Cape fur seals

tags.features: kelp forests, swim-throughs, rocky reef, Finlay's Pinnacle
tags.marine_life: bryozoans, sponges, colonial ascidians, anemones, pyjama catsharks, reef fish, nudibranchs
tags.notes: Sevengill sharks NOT confirmed as reliable sighting at this specific site — removed from marine life tag.

tags.wreck_location: Duiker Point, Sandy Bay, Cape Peninsula
tags.underlying_wreck: SS Oakburn (British cargo steamer, 1906, pre-existing wreck at same location)
tags.warning: Advanced stage of deterioration; rusted steel threatening collapse; area unsafe
tags.conditions: exposed location, strong surge in shallows; shore access via Sandy Bay beach
tags.notes: Previous description said 'capable of lifting 1,200 tons' — confirmed accurate per Wikipedia. Previous description said 'boat access from Hout Bay' — incorrect; entry_type is shore (Sandy Bay beach). Also Oakburn described as carrying 'railway lines and antique glassware' — no independent source confirms this specific cargo claim; removed.

tags.marine_life: octopus, biscuit skates, blue-fin gurnard, pineapple fish, stingrays, Cape fur seals; nearby SAS Pietermaritzburg minesweeper wreck at max 20m
tags.conditions: calm sheltered site, most protected site on Cape Peninsula
tags.notes: WWII wrecks nearby confirmed: SAS Pietermaritzburg (A-class minesweeper, D-Day veteran, max 20m). 'Pyjama sharks, gully sharks, shysharks' not confirmed at THIS specific site; removed from tag. Southern Right whale sightings in bay not confirmed at dive site specifically.

tags.features: twin reef outcrops (cow and calf), sand base at 10-12m
tags.marine_life: Cape fur seals, pyjama catsharks, spotted gully sharks, Cape knifejaw, Roman sea bream, nudibranchs, sea cucumbers, starfish, octopus
tags.notes: Sevengill sharks listed in previous version; elusive since 2016 per diveteam.co.za, removed as reliable sighting. Palmate sea fans and feather stars retained as cold-water invertebrate fauna consistent with site.

tags.features: boulder reef, Seal Rock, Big Rock, Deep Partridge (25m), Peter's Pinnacles, Gorgonian Forest
tags.marine_life: Cape fur seals, Hottentot sea bream, Fransmadam, Roman, Two-tone Fingerfin, Galjoen, klipfish, sea fans, nudibranchs, anemones, octopus
tags.site_type_correction: Corrected from 'wall' to 'reef': site consists of boulder/granite reef topography, NOT vertical wall. Multiple authoritative sources confirm reef classification.

tags.name: Antipolis
tags.sinking_cause: Tow cable snapped/snagged on seabed during northwesterly gale; crew of tug Kiyo Maru 2 forced to cut cable; both Antipolis and companion tanker Romelia blown ashore
tags.companion_vessel: MV Romelia (grounded simultaneously at Llandudno/Sunset Rocks)
tags.location: Oudekraal, Atlantic seaboard (Victoria Road, near Twelve Apostles Hotel)
tags.marine_life: cape rock lobster, algae, encrusting invertebrates; limited fish diversity per dive operators
tags.conditions: very cold water, boulder entry, offshore wind can make return swim strenuous
tags.wreck_status: partially washed further ashore in Jan 2022 storm; bow ribs near shoreline, boiler visible at low tide

tags.name: SELI1
tags.cargo: 30,000 tons coal, 660 tons heavy fuel oil, 60 tons diesel fuel
tags.cause: Engine failure and snapped anchor chain in strong westerly winds
tags.salvage: Wreckage cut and dispersed underwater; R40m removal operation completed March 2013
tags.condition: submerged wreckage on sand to ~20m; marker buoy installed 2015

tags.name: Het Huis te Kraaienstjin
tags.ship_yard: Zeeland Yard, for Zeeland Chamber of the Dutch East India Company
tags.cause: Struck rocks at Oudekraal in thick mist attempting to enter Table Bay; maiden outward voyage
tags.cargo: 19 chests of pieces-of-eight (approximately 57,000 pieces); three chests unaccounted for after the wreck
tags.location: North Oudekraal, Atlantic seaboard, Table Mountain National Park MPA
tags.cannon: Two iron cannon approximately 3m long in V-formation at southern end; two more cannon partly buried in sand
tags.artifacts: Large iron anchor nearby; scattered wood pieces
tags.notes: Difficulty upgraded from Beginner to Intermediate: cold Atlantic water, 150m swim from shore, swell possible. Cannon are IRON not bronze — previous description was incorrect. Previous description said 'anchor cable parted in fog' — corrected: actual cause was striking rocks in thick mist.

tags.name: Thomas T. Tucker
tags.cause: Compass error of 37 degrees caused by iron cargo (tanks/vehicles); captain believed ship near Robben Island; ran aground in heavy fog on maiden voyage
tags.cargo: 25 Sherman tanks, 16 tank cars, 200 motor vehicles, barbed wire; destined for British forces in North Africa via Suez
tags.route: New Orleans to Suez, maiden voyage
tags.location: Olifantsbos Point (Cape Point), Table Mountain National Park MPA
tags.conditions: extremely exposed, divable only in flat seas; site split into three sections
tags.entry: shore access on foot to Olifantsbos; NOT boat-only
tags.notes: Previous markdown description incorrectly said 'boat only' — entry is actually SHORE on foot from Olifantsbos parking area. Cargo description corrected: 25 Sherman tanks confirmed, NOT 'ammunition' as previously tagged. Compass error 37 degrees confirmed (not 23 nautical miles as stated in markdown — both may be true: 37 degree error resulting in ~23 NM position error).

tags.name: Nolloth
tags.cause: Struck Albatross Rock off Olifantsbos; captain ran her aground to save crew
tags.cargo: Mostly liquor and general goods (successfully salvaged)
tags.rescue: First helicopter rescue in SA maritime history; crew transported by helicopter to safety
tags.location: Olifantsbos, Cape Point (second wreck on Olifantsbos Shipwreck Trail)
tags.conditions: visible at high tide, walkable at low tide; surge zone; dive only in flat conditions
tags.history: First helicopter rescue in SA maritime history

tags.name: Phyllisia Shipwreck
tags.cause: Struck submerged rocks offshore near midnight; holed in four places
tags.crew: 11 came ashore in life rafts; 14 airlifted by helicopter
tags.location: Hoek van Bobbejaan (Cape Point headland), Cape Point Nature Reserve
tags.conditions: fully exposed, virtually never diveable; hull visible at low tide; access mainly on foot
tags.access_type: Phyllisia Circuit hiking trail (5km from Gifkommetjie parking area)
tags.wreck_remains: Part of stern remains on shore

tags.name: Whittle Rock
tags.pinnacle_top: 5m (shallowest pinnacle, approximately 4.4-5m below MSL depending on tide)
tags.features: granite corestone pinnacles, gullies, walls, overhangs, occasional swimthroughs; multiple named pinnacle clusters
tags.conditions: 8km offshore False Bay; exposed to southwest swell; boat access from Simon's Town jetty (14.5km) or Miller's Point (9km)
tags.marine_life: gully sharks in swimthroughs, large short-tailed stingrays, basket stars, feather stars, sea fans, sponges, yellowtail (seasonal)
tags.navigation_buoy: Cardinal East marker buoy moored at ~21m
tags.notes: Previous description mentioned 'bronze whalers and ragged-tooth sharks' — NOT confirmed at this specific site by reviewed sources; removed. Marine life updated to source-confirmed species only.

tags.name: Clan Stewart
tags.ship_name_correct: SS Clan Stuart (commonly misspelled Clan Stewart)
tags.ship_build_yard: W. Doxford and Son, Sunderland, England
tags.cause: Dragged anchor in SE gale; grounded near Glencairn; deliberately run aground at Mackerel Bay after failed refloating attempts to prevent blocking harbour approach
tags.location: Mackerel Bay, Simon's Town (~100m from Mackerel Beach)
tags.marine_life: red bait (ascidians), coralline algae, bryozoans, nudibranchs
tags.notes: Previous description said 'great white sharks have been sighted on several occasions' — NOT confirmed as specific to this site; great whites also absent from False Bay since ~2018. Statement removed. Previous description said 'squid, cuttlefish, rays, fish species' — these are not confirmed at this specific site by reviewed sources.
